title: Lastrea nervosa
taxonomy: Lastrea nervosa is classified in the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a part of the order Polypodiales. This plant is further classified as a species within the genus Lastrea, a group of ferns native to various parts of the world. The classification is based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics, which distinguish it from other fern species. Lastrea nervosa is often placed in the synonym Polypodium nervosum, but the classification may vary depending on the source. The classification system used for Lastrea nervosa is likely based on the Angiosperm Phylogeny Group III (APG III) system or the International Code of Nomenclature for algae, fungi, and plants.
uses: Lastrea nervosa, commonly known as the nervous leather fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in treating various health issues. It is believed to have a positive effect on the digestive system and can be used to treat issues like diarrhea and indigestion. The plant's extracts are also used to treat skin conditions and wounds. However, further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential side effects.

intro: Lastrea nervosa, also known as the Nerve Fern, is a type of fern native to tropical regions of the world. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and exhibit antioxidant properties.
